GDPTestGenerator (redirected from Notes.GDPTestGenerator ) The GDP is no longer supported by the Accessors Framework. This page exists as a historical reference. $PTII/ptolemy/actor/lib/jjs/modules/gdp/demo/GDPTestGenerator/GDPTestGenerator.xml is

gdp/demo/GDPTestGenerator/GDPTestGenerator.xml is a Ptolemy model that is deployed as a Swarmlet on terra.eecs.berkeley.edu that generates periodic test data. The swarmlet appends to a GDP log named "edu.berkeley.eecs.testlog" a sequence of JSON data

edu.berkeley.eecs.testlog" a sequence of JSON data. A new output is produced once per second and the values cycle through a specified range. This is intended to be a test log that can be relied to be always producing data. The JSON produced has the f

always producing data. The JSON produced has the following properties: pressure_pascals: ranges from 70,100 Pa (pressure at about 3000 m elevation) to 101,000 Pa (at sea level). huminity_precent: ranges from 0 to 100. temperature_celcius: ranges from

es from 0 to 100. temperature_celcius: ranges from 100 down to -10. light_lux: ranges from 0 (no light) to 1000 (overcast day, very bright office). battery_percent: ranges from 100 to 0. sequence_number: starting from 0, the count of outputs from an

er: starting from 0, the count of outputs from an execution of this test. time: the time at which this output is produced. Deploying on TerraSwarm The $PTII/ptolemy/actor/lib/jjs/modules/gdp/demo/GDPTestGenerator/GDPTestGenerator.xml model has an Acc

estGenerator/GDPTestGenerator.xml model has an AccessorSSHCodeGenerator that deploys the swarmlet to terra.eecs.berkeley.edu using the sbuser account. To update the version running on terra, edit the AccessorSSHCodeGenerator attribute and change the

AccessorSSHCodeGenerator attribute and change the remote host to sbuser@terra.eecs.berkeley.edu . You will also need ssh access to sbuser on terra, see Access Control And Key Distribution . The password for the sbuser@terraswarm.org account on https:

rd for the sbuser@terraswarm.org account on https://repo.eecs.berkeley.edu may be found on Terra in ~sbuser/.repopass. Then run the model, the code should be redeployed to terra. You may need to do pm2 reload 0 to reload the swarmlet. The swarmlet i

m2 reload 0 to reload the swarmlet. The swarmlet is restarted at boot time and periodically using pm2. Attach:gdpSbuserPm2.png Δ In addition, there is cronjob that restarts every 5 minutes. /home/sbuser/sbuser.cron says: PATH = / usr / local / python

er/sbuser.cron says: PATH = / usr / local / python / bin: / usr / local / sbin: / sbin: / bin: / usr / sbin: / usr / bin # # /home/sbuser/sbuser.cron # */ 5 * * * * csh -c '/home/sbuser/cg/node_modules/.bin/pm2 restart GDPTestGenerator.js' >& / home

s/.bin/pm2 restart GDPTestGenerator.js' >& / home / sbuser / sbuser.cron.log [$[Get Code]] Log Rotation We are using https://github.com/pm2-hive/pm2-logrotate pm2-logrotation : pm2 install pm2-logrotate pm2 set pm2-logrotate:rotateInterval '* * * /1

te pm2 set pm2-logrotate:rotateInterval '* * * /1 * *' [$[Get Code]] Testing Subscription There is a model at $PTII/ptolemy/actor/lib/jjs/modules/gdp/test/auto/GDPTerraLog.xml that tests out subscription. gdp-reader Here's how to test whether the edu

ion. gdp-reader Here's how to test whether the edu.berkeley.eecs.testlog is active using gdp-reader bash-3.2$ $PTII / vendors / gdp / gdp / apps / gdp-reader -s edu.berkeley.eecs.testlog Reading GCL T2bu1EV7VRFDM3Ao5ybbtWS8uxPrCOyJngCv0JMcils >>> rec

. 3 3 2 Z 000000b0 22 7d " } [$[Get Code]] WebSockets Terra is also running GDPToWebSocket.js, which should set up a WebSocket on terra that publishes the data. To run the model locally: $ PTII / bin / capecode $PTII / ptolemy / actor / lib / j

/ bin / capecode $PTII / ptolemy / actor / lib / jjs / modules / gdp / demo / GDPToWebSocket /* .xml [$[Get Code]] Run GDPToWebSocket.xml first, then TestGDPToWebSocket.xml TestGDPToWebSocket.xml should connect to the WebSocket created by GDPToWebSoc
